title: OISD-GS-1: Safety practices for hydrocarbon industries

clauses.6.3: Toxic gas monitoring: H2S detectors shall be installed in all areas where H2S concentration may exceed 1 ppm. Hot work shall not be performed when H2S exceeds 5 ppm in the work zone or within 15 metres.
clauses.6.4: Carbon monoxide monitoring: CO detectors required in all confined spaces. Work shall be suspended when CO exceeds 25 ppm.
clauses.7.1: Gas pressure management: Hot work permits shall be automatically suspended when collector main pressure exceeds the warning threshold. All permits must be re-validated after any pressure exceedance event.
clauses.5.2: Explosive atmosphere management: No ignition sources in Zone 1 when CH4 exceeds 10% LEL. Mandatory gas-free certificate before hot work.

clauses.S.36(1): No person shall be required or allowed to enter any confined space in which dangerous fumes are likely to be present unless it has been certified safe by a competent person immediately before entry.
clauses.S.36(1)(a): Atmospheric testing for oxygen, flammable, and toxic gases must be performed immediately before entry and documented in the permit-to-work system.
clauses.S.36(3): No naked flame or light likely to ignite fumes shall be used in any confined space.
clauses.S.37: Precautions against explosive or inflammable dust: where explosive dust is liable to be generated, effective measures to prevent accumulation.

title: DGFASLI operational manual OM-2023-11: Permit-to-work for chemical hazards
source: Directorate General Factory Advice Service & Labour Institutes

clauses.4.1: Permit-to-work cross-validation: All PTW systems must be validated against real-time gas monitor readings at the time of issue and at least every 30 minutes during active work.
clauses.4.3: Automatic suspension trigger: PTW shall be suspended immediately when any gas sensor within 20 metres of the work zone exceeds the warning threshold.
clauses.5.2: Shift changeover protocol: Incoming shift supervisor must review all active permits and current gas monitor trends before accepting responsibility.
clauses.6.1: Sensor maintenance: When a gas detector is taken offline for maintenance, no hot work or confined space entry shall be permitted in the coverage area without deployment of a portable backup detector.
